message:
Enforce live session expiry with in-memory timer instead of reload-only check

Previously session expiry was only validated when the page reloaded and
restoreSession() ran. A tab left open past the expiry would remain
unlocked until the next navigation or refresh.

Add an in-memory expiry timer (activeSessionExpiryTs + setTimeout) that
actively locks the archive when the session expires. Re-check expiry on
visibilitychange so tabs woken from sleep/background catch up immediately.

When the user switches storage backends (sessionStorage <-> localStorage)
during an active session, persistSession() now preserves the original
expiry timestamp instead of silently extending it by the full default
duration.

Clear the expiry timer on all lock and error-recovery paths to prevent
stale timeouts from firing after the session is already torn down.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
